gate.heading: Choose your language
gate.subheading: Select a market flag to open teacher onboarding in that language. You can still set the language you teach in separately inside the form.

chrome.brand_blurb: Submit the material Pearl_Int needs to turn a real teacher into a reviewed teacher-mode source.
chrome.activation_truth_body: Activate submits intake. Official teacher status still requires source normalization, atom mining, human review, and teacher production gates.
chrome.draft_blurb: Save anytime. You get a private link to come back and add more material — no login.

header.title: Build the source packet before the system writes in a teacher's voice.

identity.heading: Who is the teacher?
identity.language_hint: Source language for this teacher (may differ from the page display language). 14 repo locales.
identity.bio_hint: Paste text, link a URL, and/or upload a bio file — any combination works.
identity.bio_placeholder: What should Pearl_Int know about this teacher's work, background, audience, and voice?

rights.heading: What are we allowed to process?
rights.owns_material: I own or have permission to submit these teachings, recordings, files, and links for processing.
rights.processing_consent: I consent to Pearl_Int extracting candidate teacher atoms for human review before production use.
rights.no_unauthorized: I am not submitting unauthorized copyrighted material, and any public excerpts have rights/provenance notes.
rights.final_consent: I confirm this intake can be submitted for operator review and future teacher-source activation work.
rights.public_placeholder: What can be quoted, paraphrased, or used publicly?
rights.private_placeholder: What should never be used, named, mined, or surfaced?

teachings.heading: Give us the teacher's doctrine and teaching material.
teachings.blurb: Add as many teaching blocks as you need. Inside each block you can paste many teachings (separate with blank lines), attach a URL, and/or upload files.
teachings.hint: Tip: one paragraph or blank-line-separated block per teaching works best.

stories.heading: Supply real story material, not tiny examples.
stories.blurb: Pick a story type, then paste text, link a source URL, and/or upload. Start with three rows — add more anytime.
stories.placeholder: What happens? Scene, pressure, friction, choice, consequence, teaching function…

practices.heading: Add exercises and tools with enough context to become five-layer atoms.
practices.blurb: Same pattern: type · text · URL · upload. Three rows to start.
practices.placeholder: Steps, timing, body cues, aha, integration, contraindications, safety notes…

voice.heading: Quotes, phrases, metaphors, and language boundaries.
voice.blurb: Each row is one language category. Use text, URL, and/or upload — add more rows as needed.
voice.source_placeholder: Where this came from / usage permission
voice.content_placeholder: Paste content for this language entry…

activate.heading: Submit the teacher intake packet.
activate.ready_body: We'll save your draft, submit intake for review, and give you a private URL so you can come back and add more anytime.
activate.depth_blurb: You can activate with less than the preferred counts — this is guidance, not a hard block.
activate.submit_failed: Activation failed. Try again or save a draft first.

success.heading: Congratulations
success.body_suffix: was submitted. Official teacher status still needs Pearl_Int review — you can keep adding material anytime with your private link.
success.no_url: Save a draft next time so we can issue a private return link. Your intake was still recorded.

draft.saved: Draft saved. Keep your private resume link.
draft.delete_confirm: Delete this draft? Submissions are never deleted from this action.

validation.owns_required: Own-voice or permission attestation is required.
validation.processing_required: Permission to process material into candidate atoms is required.
validation.material_required: Add at least one teaching, story, practice, language row, or bio (text, URL, or upload).
